domain_signal.summary: Modernized US bank with substantial public engineering activity. Banking product itself is regulated and partner-gated, but their published OSS, talks, and blog reveal deep investment in cloud governance, data quality/profiling, ML reproducibility, and applied AI research at scale.

domain_evidence.claim: April 2026 blog posts on NVIDIA GTC, ICLR, AI Symposium, Spark executor tuning
DataProfiler ~1.5k stars, datacompy ~640 stars — meaningful OSS adoption in data-quality space
Slingshot product publicly shipped with Python SDK targeting Snowflake/Databricks cost optimization

apis.endpoint_family: DevExchange (Credit Offers, Customer Transactions, Enhanced Decisioning, Prefill, Retrieve Consumer Bank Products)
apis.notes: Listed publicly on developer.capitalone.com but require contract-gated onboarding. Not callable by an arbitrary developer.
Endpoint offline as of discovery; not usable.

sdks.notes: Targets Slingshot only — cloud cost optimization for Snowflake/Databricks. Not a banking SDK.

notable_oss_authored.name: Cloud Custodian
notable_oss_authored.now_owned_by: CNCF (creator Kapil Thangavelu left for Stacklet)

engineering_practices.notes: Per the OSS-as-signal reframe: this is the richest dimension for Capital One. The bank product is locked, but the OSS portfolio reveals exactly what their platform engineering org cares about — and those domains (cloud governance, data quality, ML reproducibility) are themselves agent-skill-shaped problems.

company_ai_posture.notes: Heavy AI research investment per recent blog: DynaGuard, M4-RAG (multimodal retrieval), MR3 (multilingual reward modeling), VLM robustness research. Ships AI in their own internal DevEx tooling. No public agent-facing surface yet.

agentic_landscape.competition_assessment: Greenfield at the agent layer. No MCP exists for Slingshot, Cloud Custodian, DataProfiler, datacompy, or rubicon-ml. Any skill built here is the first in its niche.

people_signal.name: George Mao
people_signal.current_focus: Serverless / AWS Lambda at Capital One scale (InfoQ talk)

people_signal.name: Kapil Thangavelu
people_signal.role: Creator of Cloud Custodian (former Capital One)
people_signal.current_focus: Cloud Custodian commercialization at Stacklet — flagged because his departure means Cloud Custodian's primary maintainer is no longer at CapOne

access_classification.surface: Capital One GitHub OSS (DataProfiler, datacompy, rubicon-ml, locopy, etc.)
DevExchange banking APIs (Credit Offers, Customer Transactions, etc.)
Capital One UK Open Banking APIs

skill_targets.title: Cloud cost governance with Capital One Slingshot
skill_targets.rationale: Real product, public Python SDK (v2.1.0), concrete agent-shaped task: query Snowflake/Databricks usage, identify cost anomalies, recommend optimizations. No competing skill exists.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: First Slingshot-aware skill; lift comes from teaching the SDK contract + cost-analysis idioms.

skill_targets.title: Data quality workflows with DataProfiler + datacompy
skill_targets.rationale: These are the two highest-starred Capital One OSS projects, both in the data-quality space. Agents working with tabular data routinely need profiling and comparison; a skill that knows the idioms of both libraries (and when to use which) directly serves an underserved niche.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: OSS-as-signal target: skill is for any developer using these tools, not exclusively Capital One employees. Maximum reach because the tools have community traction.

skill_targets.title: Cloud governance via Cloud Custodian policies
skill_targets.rationale: Cloud Custodian is the highest-impact OSS Capital One originated, but it now lives at CNCF and has its own community. Building a 'Capital One' skill for it is borderline mis-attributed.
skill_targets.existing_competition: Cloud Custodian's own docs + Stacklet's commercial offering.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: Probably better attributed as a CNCF-Cloud-Custodian skill than a Capital One skill. Flag for human review of attribution.

skill_targets.title: ML experiment tracking with rubicon-ml
skill_targets.rationale: Lower stars than DataProfiler/datacompy but a real, maintained CapOne-owned tool in the experiment-tracking niche (competes loosely with MLflow/W&B).
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: Narrow but defensible — niche tool, no agent skill exists.
